results.name: max_run_b_choice_correlation
results.description: Pearson correlation between max_run_norm_b and chose_left; tests whether longer B runs monotonically increase the probability of choosing A, probing max-run as a salience cue absent from the model.

results.name: low_max_run_b_choice_rate
results.description: Mean chose_left when max_run_norm_b < 0.2 (B has only very short runs, appears locally balanced); tests if humans judge B as more random when it lacks salient streaks.

results.name: head_imbalance_b_choice_rate
results.description: Mean chose_left when imbalance_b > 0.3 and imbalance_a < 0.1 (B is head-biased, A is balanced); tests calibration of the biased-coin component by isolating trials where B's head proportion clearly departs from 0.5.

results.name: high_rep_motifs_b_choice_rate
results.description: Mean chose_left when rep_motifs_b >= 3 (B contains many repeated sub-sequences); tests whether humans use repetitive-motif count as a non-randomness cue that the model omits.

results.name: high_alt_b_choice_rate
results.description: Mean chose_left when p_alts_b > 0.65 (B is highly alternating); tests whether the model correctly predicts choice direction when B switches flanks at a high rate.

results.name: alt_rate_diff_choice_correlation
results.description: Pearson correlation between (p_alts_b - p_alts_a) and chose_left across all trials; tests the sign and slope of the alternation-rate contrast on choice, probing whether the model's alternation-penalization is directionally correct.

results.name: low_alt_b_choice_rate
results.description: Mean chose_left when p_alts_b < 0.45 (B is streaky, few alternations); tests whether the model correctly predicts choice when B contains long same-symbol runs.

results.name: high_max_run_b_choice_rate
results.description: Mean chose_left when max_run_norm_b > 0.4 (B has a long unbroken run relative to its length); tests if the model captures run-length salience as a non-randomness cue.
